pathways.title: Thermodynamic headroom
pathways.action: Maintain reserve cadence and keep Monte Carlo breach probability below tolerance.

pathways.title: Mission Hamiltonian stability
pathways.action: Reduce mission load or extend slack windows until stability clears 85%.

pathways.title: Nash deviation control
pathways.action: Keep incentive gradients aligned with Nash stability targets.

pathways.title: Allocation diversification
pathways.action: Maintain diversified allocation weights to avoid concentration risk.

pathways.title: Sentient coalition balance
pathways.action: Continue cooperative reward rotations to sustain coalition stability.

pathways.title: Logistics game-theory slack
pathways.action: Maintain corridor utilisation within the equilibrium band.

pathways.title: Compute quorum resilience
pathways.action: Sustain quorum failover coverage and monitor deviation drift.

actionPath.title: Capacity spillover capture
actionPath.action: Expand shard energy feeds or rebalance weights so free energy is absorbed without breaching per-shard capacity limits.

actionPath.title: Mission Hamiltonian load-balance
actionPath.target: Mission Hamiltonian stability ≥ 90% with ≥ 5% free energy headroom.
actionPath.action: Shift energy-intensive programmes, stretch critical-path timelines, and lower risk exposure until mission stability clears target.

actionPath.title: Logistics entropy smoothing
actionPath.action: Shift corridor quotas toward the 0.75 equilibrium band to maximize logistics entropy and game-theory slack.

actionPath.title: Thermodynamic headroom reset
actionPath.target: Free energy margin ≥ 8%, runway ≥ 1h, and Hamiltonian stability ≥ 90%.
actionPath.rationale: Gibbs free energy 4220725703.7 GJ · Hamiltonian 83.7% · runway 1.08h
actionPath.action: Increase Dyson reserve buffers, dampen demand variance, and re-run the Monte Carlo sweep until breach probability clears tolerance.

recommendations: Raise logistics entropy ratio above 0.90 by smoothing corridor utilization bands.
Rebalance mission timelines and energy allocations to raise mission Hamiltonian stability.
